title: HoleFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a hole feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setAllExtent
methods.description: Defines the extent of the hole to be through-all. The direction can be either positive, negative.

methods.name: setDistanceExtent
methods.description: Defines the depth of the hole using a specified distance.

methods.name: setLengthAndOffset
methods.description: Sets the length and offset of the thread of a tapped hole.   This method is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise calling this method will fail.   By default the isFullLength property is true which means the thread is the full length of the hole and there is no offset. Calling this method will have the side effect of setting the isFullLength property to false.

methods.name: setOneSideToExtent
methods.description: Sets the extent of the hole to be from the sketch plane to the specified "to" face.

methods.name: setPositionAtCenter
methods.description: Defines the position of the hole at the center of a circular or elliptical edge of the face.

methods.name: setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets
methods.description: Defines the orientation of the hole using a planar face or construction plane. The position of the hole is defined by the distance from one or two edges.

methods.name: setPositionByPoint
methods.description: Defines the position of a the hole using a point. The point can be a vertex on the face or it can be a Point3D object to define any location on the face. If a Point3D object is provided it will be projected onto the plane along the planes normal. The orientation of the hole is defined by the planar face or construction plane. If a vertex is used, the position of the hole is associative to that vertex. If a Point3D object is used the position of the hole is not associative.

methods.name: setPositionBySketchPoint
methods.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole using a sketch point.

methods.name: setPositionBySketchPoints
methods.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole using a set of sketch points.

methods.name: setPositionOnEdge
methods.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole to be on the start, end or center of an edge.

methods.name: setToClearanceHole
methods.description: Sets the hole to be a clearance hole of the size specified by the ClearanceHoleInfo object.

methods.name: setToSimpleHole
methods.description: This property sets the hole's tap to be "simple", which means that it will not have any tap and will be a simple hole. When a new input is created, it defaults to being a simple hole.

methods.name: setToTappedHole
methods.description: Sets the hole to be a straight or tapered tapped hole of the size specified by the ThreadInfo object.

properties.name: creationOccurrence
properties.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the Hole is created based on geometry (e.g. a face or point) in another component AND (the Hole) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI A value of null indicates that everything is in the context of a single component.

properties.name: holeTapType
properties.description: Returns the current type of tap associated with this hole. When a new HoleFeatureInput is created, this will default to SimpleHoleTapType, which means the hole will not have any tap and will be a simple hole. You can set the tap type by using one of the methods to define the specific tap desired.

properties.name: isDefaultDirection
properties.description: Gets or sets if the hole goes in the default direction or is reversed.

properties.name: isFullLength
properties.description: Gets and sets if this thread is the full length of the hole. It defaults to true.   This property is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise this property is ignored.   The property can only be set to True, which will cause the feature to ignore the values of the threadLength and threadOffset properties. Using the setLengthAndOffset method will have the side effect of setting this property to false.

properties.name: isModeled
properties.description: Gets and sets if the thread is physical or cosmetic thread. A value of true indicates a physical thread. It defaults to false.   This property is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise this property is ignored.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: participantBodies
properties.description: Gets and sets the list of bodies that will participate in the hole.   If this property has not been set, the default behavior is that all bodies that are intersected by the hole will participate.   This property can return null in the case where the feature has not been fully defined so that possible intersecting bodies can be computed.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

properties.name: threadLength
properties.description: Gets the thread length when the isFullLength property is False. Returns null when the isFullLength property is true.

properties.name: threadOffset
properties.description: Gets the thread offset when the isFullLength property is False. Returns null when the isFullLength property is true.

properties.name: tipAngle
properties.description: Gets the ValueInput object that defines the angle of the tip of the hole. The default is "118.0 deg" but can be modified by setting it using another Value object.

related_samples.name: Hole Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new hole feature.

classType.title: HoleFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.HoleFeatureInput.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::HoleFeatureInput::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setAllExtent.title: HoleFeatureInput.setAllExtent Method
setAllExtent.description: Defines the extent of the hole to be through-all. The direction can be either positive, negative.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setAllExtent(direction)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setAllExtent(direction);

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction of the hole relative to the normal of the sketch plane.

setDistanceExtent.title: HoleFeatureInput.setDistanceExtent Method
setDistanceExtent.description: Defines the depth of the hole using a specified distance.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setDistanceExtent(distance)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setDistanceExtent(distance);

setDistanceExtent.return_description: Returns true if setting the extent was successful.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: The depth of the hole. If a real is specified the value is in centimeters. If a string is specified the units are derived from the string. If no units are specified, the default units of the document are used.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createSimpleInput method. To use this sample, have a design open with a box. Select the face for the hole and two edges to define the position of the hole.

related_samples.name: Hole Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new hole feature.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add with Counterbore
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createCounterboreInput method. The hole is positioned at the center of the selected edge.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add with Countersink
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createCountersinkInput method and postions the hole in the center of a selected circular edge.

setLengthAndOffset.title: HoleFeatureInput.setLengthAndOffset Method
setLengthAndOffset.description: Sets the length and offset of the thread of a tapped hole. This method is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise calling this method will fail. By default the isFullLength property is true which means the thread is the full length of the hole and there is no offset. Calling this method will have the side effect of setting the isFullLength property to false.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setLengthAndOffset(length, offset)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setLengthAndOffset(length, offset);

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Sets the length of the thread.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Sets the offset of the thread from the start of the hole. A value of zero is valid for no offset.

setOneSideToExtent.title: HoleFeatureInput.setOneSideToExtent Method
setOneSideToExtent.description: Sets the extent of the hole to be from the sketch plane to the specified "to" face.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity, matchShape)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity, matchShape, directionHint)

parameters.name: toEntity
parameters.description: The entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a hole it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.

parameters.name: matchShape
parameters.description: Indicates if the hole is not contained on the face that the hole should match the shape of the entity as if it extended beyond its current boundaries.

parameters.name: directionHint
parameters.description: Specifies the direction of the hole. This is only used in the case where there are two possible solutions and the hole can hit the toEntity in either direction.   Typically there is only a single solution and the direction is determined automatically.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setPositionAtCenter.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionAtCenter Method
setPositionAtCenter.description: Defines the position of the hole at the center of a circular or elliptical edge of the face.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionAtCenter(planarEntity, centerEdge)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setPositionAtCenter(planarEntity, centerEdge);

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or ConstructionPlane object that defines the orientation of the hole. The natural direction of the hole will be opposite the normal of the face or construction plane.

parameters.name: centerEdge
parameters.description: A circular or elliptical edge whose center point will be the position of the hole.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add with Countersink
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createCountersinkInput method and postions the hole in the center of a selected circular edge.

setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets Method
setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets.description: Defines the orientation of the hole using a planar face or construction plane. The position of the hole is defined by the distance from one or two edges.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets(planarEntity, point, edgeOne, offsetOne)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionByPlaneAndOffsets(planarEntity, point, edgeOne, offsetOne, edgeTwo, offsetTwo)

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or ConstructionPlane object that defines the orientation of the hole. The natural direction of the hole will be opposite the normal of the face or construction plane.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: A Point3D object that defines the approximate initial position of the hole. The point will be projected onto the plane. This point should be close to the final position of the hole and is used to determine which solution out of several possible solutions should be chosen for the hole location.   This point is defined in the coordinate system of the native entity used for the planarEntity argument. For example, if the planarEntity argument is a proxy to a BRepFace, the point argument must be in the coordinate system of the component of the native face, not the proxy.

parameters.name: edgeOne
parameters.description: A linear BRepEdge object that the position of the hole will be measured from. The position of the hole will be measured along a perpendicular from this edge.

parameters.name: offsetOne
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the offset distance from edgeOne. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "3 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: edgeTwo
parameters.description: You can optionally define a second edge and offset to specify the position of the hole. If you use a second edge it has the same requirements as the edgeOne argument. If you provide a second edge you must also provide the offsetTwo argument.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

parameters.name: offsetTwo
parameters.description: If edgeTwo is defined, you must provide this argument which is a ValueInput object that defines the offset from the edgeTwo. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "3 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createSimpleInput method. To use this sample, have a design open with a box. Select the face for the hole and two edges to define the position of the hole.

setPositionByPoint.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionByPoint Method
setPositionByPoint.description: Defines the position of a the hole using a point. The point can be a vertex on the face or it can be a Point3D object to define any location on the face. If a Point3D object is provided it will be projected onto the plane along the planes normal. The orientation of the hole is defined by the planar face or construction plane. If a vertex is used, the position of the hole is associative to that vertex. If a Point3D object is used the position of the hole is not associative.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionByPoint(planarEntity, point)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setPositionByPoint(planarEntity, point);

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or ConstructionPlane object that defines the orientation of the hole. The natural direction of the hole will be opposite the normal of the face or construction plane.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: A Point3D object or vertex that defines the position of the hole. The point will be projected onto the plane along the normal of the plane.

setPositionBySketchPoint.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionBySketchPoint Method
setPositionBySketchPoint.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole using a sketch point.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionBySketchPoint(sketchPoint)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setPositionBySketchPoint(sketchPoint);

parameters.name: sketchPoint
parameters.description: The sketch point that defines the position of the hole. The orientation is inferred from the normal of the point's parent sketch. The natural direction will be opposite the normal of the sketch.

setPositionBySketchPoints.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionBySketchPoints Method
setPositionBySketchPoints.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole using a set of sketch points.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionBySketchPoints(sketchPoints)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setPositionBySketchPoints(sketchPoints);

parameters.name: sketchPoints
parameters.description: A collection of sketch points that defines the positions of the holes. The orientation is inferred from the normal of the point's parent sketch. The natural direction will be opposite the normal of the sketch. The points can be from multiple sketches but they must all be co-planar.

related_samples.name: Hole Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new hole feature.

setPositionOnEdge.title: HoleFeatureInput.setPositionOnEdge Method
setPositionOnEdge.description: Defines the position and orientation of the hole to be on the start, end or center of an edge.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setPositionOnEdge(planarEntity, edge, position)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setPositionOnEdge(planarEntity, edge, position);

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or ConstructionPlane object that defines the orientation of the hole and start of the hole. The natural direction of the hole will be opposite the normal of the face or construction plane.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.description: The edge to position the hole on.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: The position along the edge to place the hole.

related_samples.name: holeFeatures.add with Counterbore
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the holeFeatures.add method using the createCounterboreInput method. The hole is positioned at the center of the selected edge.

setToClearanceHole.title: HoleFeatureInput.setToClearanceHole Method
setToClearanceHole.description: Sets the hole to be a clearance hole of the size specified by the ClearanceHoleInfo object.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setToClearanceHole(clearanceHoleInfo)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setToClearanceHole(clearanceHoleInfo);

setToClearanceHole.return_description: Returns true if setting to a clearance hole was successful.

parameters.name: clearanceHoleInfo
parameters.description: The ClearanceHoleInfo object that specifies the size of the clearance hole.

setToSimpleHole.title: HoleFeatureInput.setToSimpleHole Method
setToSimpleHole.description: This property sets the hole's tap to be "simple", which means that it will not have any tap and will be a simple hole. When a new input is created, it defaults to being a simple hole.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setToSimpleHole()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setToSimpleHole();

setToTappedHole.title: HoleFeatureInput.setToTappedHole Method
setToTappedHole.description: Sets the hole to be a straight or tapered tapped hole of the size specified by the ThreadInfo object.

syntax.python: returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var.setToTappedHole(threadInfo)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = holeFeatureInput_var->setToTappedHole(threadInfo);

setToTappedHole.return_description: Returns true if setting to a tapped hole was successful.

parameters.name: threadInfo
parameters.description: The ThreadInfo object that specifies the thread to use for the tapped hole. Whether it is straight or tapered tap is defined by the input ThreadInfo object.

creationOccurrence.title: HoleFeatureInput.creationOccurrence Property
creationOccurrence.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the Hole is created based on geometry (e.g. a face or point) in another component AND (the Hole) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI A value of null indicates that everything is in the context of a single component.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = holeFeatureInput_var.creationOccurrence

# Set the value of the property.
holeFeatureInput_var.creationOccurrence = propertyValue

When a new HoleFeatureInput is created, this will default to SimpleHoleTapType, which means the hole will not have any tap and will be a simple hole. isFullLength.title: HoleFeatureInput.isFullLength Property
isFullLength.description: Gets and sets if this thread is the full length of the hole. It defaults to true. This property is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise this property is ignored. The property can only be set to True, which will cause the feature to ignore the values of the threadLength and threadOffset properties. Using the setLengthAndOffset method will have the side effect of setting this property to false.

isModeled.title: HoleFeatureInput.isModeled Property
isModeled.description: Gets and sets if the thread is physical or cosmetic thread. A value of true indicates a physical thread. It defaults to false. This property is only used when creating a tapped hole, which means the setToTappedHole method has been called. Otherwise this property is ignored.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

# Set the value of the property.
